article_meta.title: — News —Study Shows Pandemic Saw Rise In Firearm Injuries Among KidsBy Charlotte Pence Bond•Dec 20, 2022 DailyWire.com•F

reasoning: The summary uses neutral, attributed language and avoids the source’s loaded framing (e.g., it omits “disastrous effect”). Claims are tied to studies or expert attribution. Minor mitigating context from the article (declines for some groups since 1999) is omitted, but this does not reflect adoption of biased framing.

custom_reasoning.spin: Verbs are descriptive and neutral (found, analyzed, attribute).
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All key claims are linked to named studies, data, or quoted experts.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluations are attributed to researchers/experts, not stated as facts by the model.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No heightened or alarming language beyond factual statements.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or mental states without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: No competing sides presented; neutral reporting of findings.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits article’s note on declines for certain groups since 1999, reducing balance.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Avoids subjective descriptors; uses clinical terms.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on increases and harms; excludes mitigating decline trends noted in article.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Relies on institutional sources proportionate to the article; no undue deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Removes the article’s loaded phrases (e.g., ‘disastrous’), sticking to data.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers main studies and expert comments but leaves out counter-trend details for some demographics.
